segments.text:
ENDOCYTOSIS
Uptake of external material in vesicles
Phagocytosis:  ingestion of large particles (e.g., whole bacteria)
Pinocytosis: ingestion of fluids
Receptor-mediated endocytosis: ingestion of specific molecules

RECEPTOR-MEDIATED ENDOCYTOSIS
Membrane receptors bind to specific ligands and enrich these in clathrin-coated vesicles
Examples: LDL and transferrin

LDL
LDL: low density lipoprotein
cholesterol molecules in center
outside
phospholipids
cholesterol
apolipoprotein B
Apolipoprotein B

LDL RECEPTOR
Binding to ApoB regulated by pH
Cell surface (pH 7) –  ligand-binding arm binds ApoB
LE (pH 5) – ligand-binding arm binds b-propeller and releases LDL
pH 7
pH 5

RME OF TRANSFERRIN/IRON
Ligand (transferrin) remains bound to receptor
Fe3+ released at low pH in LE

MICHAEL BROWN AND JOE GOLDSTEIN
1985 Nobel Prize
Discovered LDL receptor
Characterized RME process
